para_trans: Non-Government Aided Colleges
Conversion to private universities.
Committee set up to decide policy
about doing it.
Government of Maharashtra
Department of Higher and Technical Education
Government Decision No: Narrow-2026 / E-1549052 / VISHI-4
Martyr Rajguru Chowk, Madam Cama Marg
Mantralaya, Mumbai: 400032.
Date: April 28, 2026.
Read: - 1) Government Decision No. Narrow-2017 / (144/17) / VISHI-4 dated 19th January, 2019.
The Maharashtra Private Universities (Establishment and Regulation) Act, 2023.
The Maharashtra Private Universities (Establishment and Regulation) Rules, 2024.
The Proposal: -
With a view to provide greater autonomy to higher education institutions in the state,
Conversion of Non-Governmental Aided Colleges into Private Universities
As the demand / query is from educational institutions, academic, technical, financial
And it is necessary to formulate a comprehensive policy taking into account the administrative aspects.
In this regard, the government was considering setting up a committee to study the subject matter in detail and submit recommendations to the government.
The government's decision:
Conversion of Non-Government Aided Colleges into Private Universities.
The following committees are being constituted to study policy-making:
It is -
table_trans: Dr. Bhushan Patwardhan, Former Vice-Chairman, University Grants Commission
Deputy Secretary (University Education), Department of Higher and Technical Education, Mantralaya, Mumbai
Director of Higher Education, Maharashtra State, Pune
Joint Director, Higher Education, Pune Department, Pune

para_trans: (2) The functions of the Committee -
The functions of the Committee shall be as follows: -
(1) Necessary for conversion of aided colleges into universities.
Criteria for minimum academic performance, infrastructure, and quality of research
to decide.
(2) If land has been made available by the Government to the aided college, it shall be
Setting a policy on land.
(3) After conversion of aided colleges into private universities, by the Government.
Salary and non-salary subsidies as well as Employees' Provident Fund.
explaining the policy.
(4) Policy on Consent of Teachers / Non-Teaching Staff in Aided Colleges.
to decide.
(5) Maharashtra Public Universities Act 2016 and Maharashtra Private Universities
Framework by studying the provisions of the (Establishment and Regulation) Act 2023
to create.
(6) Reservation of backward class students and general, even after becoming a private university.
Suggesting guidelines on fee control for students.
(7) the conditions of service, conditions of service of the existing teaching and non-teaching staff of the college; and
To make recommendations on how their rights can be protected.
(8) Multidisciplinary institutions as per the National Education Policy (NEP 2020)
To examine the feasibility of such a conversion from the point of view of setting up.
(9) In accordance with the aforesaid Committee, after considering in detail the various issues, the
The committee will submit its report to the government within two months.
3. The decision of the Government of Maharashtra.
